for_c_level: This campaign represents a high-risk failure in our CI pipeline, blocking validation of ai-memory v0.6.2-rc.0 for production; readiness is stalled until harness issues are resolved. No customer-facing claims can be made about reliability improvements versus prior runs. Overall, this halts progress on federated agent memory sharing.
for_non_technical: This test run didn't work at all because no results came back from any of the planned checks. That means we couldn't confirm if agents can reliably share memories with each other. It's like trying to see if friends remember shared stories, but the whole conversation got lost.
for_sme: The entire campaign aborted without recovering any scenario reports, impacting all requested scenarios (1 through 42, partial list) across federation mesh with TLS. Probable root cause is a harness failure in report aggregation, as evidenced by empty scenarios array and 'no scenario reports recovered' reason; no specific S# or F# identifiers available due to total absence of results. This affects all primitives under test in the 4-node topology (W=2/N=4).
headline: Campaign failed with no scenario reports recovered.
next_run_change: Debug and fix the report recovery mechanism in the CI harness before re-running the campaign.
verdict: FAIL — no reports recovered from any requested scenario.
what_it_proved: The run proved a critical issue in the testing harness or infrastructure, as no scenario results were captured or reported.
what_it_tested: No scenarios were exercised due to failure in recovering reports, despite requesting 35 scenarios covering various transport, framework, and primitive axes.
